articles.title: Japan issues an advisory for northern coastal areas for a slightly increased risk of a mega-quake

The Cabinet Office and the Japan Meteorological Agency said there is 1% chance for a mega-quake occurring on the northern Japanese co

articles.title: Cuts at Global Affairs Canada marks an 'end of Pearson's dream,' report says - Ottawa Citizen
articles.description: Cuts at Global Affairs Canada marks an 'end of Pearson's dream,' report says  Ottawa Citizen

articles.title: Should public servants apply for early retirement incentives or volunteer to be laid off?
articles.description: A former executive explains the choice at hand as thousands of public servants consider applying for early retirement incentives.

articles.title: Cuts at Global Affairs Canada marks an 'end of Pearson's dream,' report says
articles.description: Budget cuts at GAC could "shift Canada's foreign policy focus away from peacekeeping and development to self-interested trade and defence."

articles.title: Water levels in Gatineau expected to peak as spring flood concerns grow
articles.description: GATINEAU — Dozens of homes and streets are at risk of flooding as water levels on the Ottawa River are predicted to rise this morning by roughly 30 centimetres. Emergency officials in Gatineau, Que., have said 164 homes are at risk of flooding and 41 streets are closed as crews respond to the threat

articles.title: Federal pause on gasoline, diesel tax takes effect today, after Iran fuel price spike
articles.description: OTTAWA — Ottawa’s temporary suspension of some fuel taxes kicks in today, with Canadians likely to save 10 cents per litre on regular gasoline, and four cents on a litre of diesel. Prime Minister Mark Carney had announced last week a pause on those fuel excise taxes up until Labour Day. The Li

articles.title: Gov. Gen. Mary Simon at United Nations to talk Indigenous rights
articles.description: OTTAWA — Gov. Gen. Mary Simon and other Indigenous leaders are heading to the United Nations on Monday to stress the importance of its Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples. articles.title: N.B. Premier Susan Holt to meet with Mark Carney and his ministers this week
articles.description: OTTAWA — New Brunswick Premier Susan Holt will be in Ottawa today to discuss the economy, defence and major projects with Prime Minister Mark Carney and his ministers. Holt says she also plans to press for federal partnership on health-care funding, energy infrastructure and critical mineral develop

articles.title: Postal workers start voting on 5-year contract, strike mandate after labour strife
articles.description: OTTAWA — Canada Post workers start voting today on a five-year contract agreement that follows labour strife and their union leader urging they reject the deal. The 55,000 employees represented by Canadian Union of Postal Workers can vote starting today until May 30 on whether to ratify an agreement
